授業目的: This course applies major economic analysis techniques to the business and industry. It is designed to provide students with a thorough understanding of business economics concepts. As such, the emphasis of the course is to learn how to use basic business economics concepts as a managerial tool to improve business decisions efficiency and profitability of firms.
到達目標: 1. To develop fundamental-level understanding of business economics;2. To introduce tools and models that can facilitate analysis of real life business decision issues;3. To gain an appreciation of how firms make economic decisions to achieve their objectives;4. To prepare students to critically think about topics related to monopoly markets, oligopoly, competitive strategy.
授業の概要・背景:
This course deals with economic issues which are related to many industries and firms. It provides
students with perspectives of economic and business decisions.
授業方法: Face to face class format.Lectures: The Instructor will give lectures on major concepts, issues and models. Case studies are provided.The teaching and learning materials are mainly based on the textbook and supplementary readings. However, we will constantly use real world cases and newspaper articles for discussion in the class. Lecture notes and course materials will be uploaded on the course website or through email.There will be one individual presentation and one in-class written exam (in the last class).

第1回:
Introduction of the course

Business economics: an overview
第6回:
Four types of Market structure:

1. Perfectly competitive markets
第10回:
Review of the course's key learning points and topics
In-class examination
第11回: Selected topic: Business and firm's competitive strategy (1)
第12回: Selected topic: Business and firm's competitive strategy (2)
第13回: Introduction of geopolitical risks and supply chain management in Asia

教科書/Required texts0:
For those with no economics study experience (highly recommended):
Dean Garratt, Elizabeth Jones, John Sloman, Jon Guest (2023). Economics for Business, 9th Edition. Pearson.
For those with a degree in economics (recommended):
Joseph Nellis and David Parker (2006). Principles of Business Economics, 2nd Edition. Pearson Education.
Jeffrey M. Perloff (2008). Microeconomics: Theory and Applications with Calculus. Pearson International Edition.
